title: Braun KG7070 Burr Grinder, Stainless Steel
description:
About this item
Grind It your way 9 main grind settings and 6 intermediate options let you fine-tune your beans for drip Coffee espresso French press or other craft coffee–style drinks
Better than blades The Stainless Steel flat burr design ensures a professional-quality uniform grind for consistent results and long-lasting performance
Flavor is always protected thanks to the professional burr grinder technology and overheat-protection signal bean quality and flavor are protected throughout the grinding process
No more counting scoops the individual cup Selector and removable grounds container let you precisely grind and pour 2 to 12 cups directly into your Coffee maker
Easy-clean design rounded edges an antistatic coating a removable top burr and container and a specially designed brush Make this one of the easiest grinders to clean
Easy on auto-off grinding starts with the push of a button and powers off automatically when the wheels stop moving
The Benchmark for design and utility for generations Braun grinders have been favored for sleek design and ease of use Today’s Braun builds on its heritage with a bold modern spin
Coffee bean hopper holds 16oz of whole beans
reviews_5s:
Play Video
After searching for a long time, I decided to go for this grinder even if it didn't have many reviews. The ones that got were positive.
I can say that I am pretty happy with my purchase.
It is very easy to use. Easy to clean and it works for my espresso machine.
I was looking for an espresso coffee grinder. Even it is not the finest l've seen, it is fine enough to make espresso on my Delonghi Machine.
I have only tried one kind of coffee beans, so I couldn't say how well it works with different kinds.
But I am pretty happy with the way it works and the quality of the product.
It does get a little bit of a static because the cup it is plastic. So it is normal to do so. But it is not messy at all.
I also like it is not loud. It makes noise because it is a machine, but not loud at all.
I made a very good choice.Play Video
I had my Kaiser grinder( made by an Korean factory which is exactly same with Delonghi DCG-4 copied from Braun KMM10) for 20 years and it is gradually dysfuntional. I accidentally found this Braun KG7070 as the new generation of previous Braun classic grinders. Immediately place the order and I am so happy to have this perfect grinder now. Here are some pros
1. low noise
2. beautiful & modern design
3. 18 grind setting
4. very even grind
5. made of solid material
6. space to store cord
7. high efficiency grinding
8. detach either up burr or powder storage bin, grinder won’t start as a safety security.

I will 100% recommend this perfect grinder and use it for the next 20 years.
** Funny matter: It’s made in China and shipped to USA, then I bought it and shipped to Taiwan. What a international trade and long way detour.Play Video
Product review after 10 days of daily usage
After doing hours of extensive coffee grinder research I decided to stay under $50 for my personal use. First let me say that any other grinder for under $50 has worse reviews. However you will find cheap blade grinder with better reviews - Why? because anyone using a cheapo blade grinder knows nothing about grinding coffee. Blade Grinder just hake your beans to bits and size of grinds will not come close to what a burr grinder does.
The Braun KG7070 does a great job at grinding beans from fine to coarse size.
2nd:The Braun grinder is very quite when compared to all other grinders under $50 .
3rd :This grinder looks great and take up very little space on the counter.
4th: Its very easy to use.
5th: Unlike other negative reviews the Braun KG7070 made almost no mess when used properly
6th: Dumping grinds into coffee makers basket was a breeze!

I would highly recommend the Braun KG7070 Stainless grinder if you are looking for great coffee grinder under $50 that looks great in your kitchen!Good grind quality, sturdy. Slightly expensive but worth it. No problems.It is an interesting machine. If you make a four cup pot then the bin above the grinder would probably be useful. I usually make 8 cups and so have to refill it every time which is not that big a problem, it does kind of mitigate the convenience of the control to set the number of cups. The choice of grinds is a nice feature and there is a clear difference in grind. It is handy if I want to make up a small second pot in my French press. Cleaning the little catch bin is easy and disassembling the grinder is not too hard. If I have an problem it is with transferring the coffee grounds from the catch bin to my coffee maker. It is perhaps a little bigger than ideal because I usually scatter grounds and so have taken to doing it over the sink which is a little convenient. However, I am glad I bought it and bought another as a gift. I had become very frustrated with the coffee I was making at home but with the improved grind, the coffee is much better.Love not having to measure and grind everyday. Just push start and perfect amount for my 8oz cup of joe! Quiet, easy to clean compact and holds a week's worth of beans for one cuppers! Great quality for the price! I pull the cup that holds the ground coffee out slowly and open slowly and I don't get coffee all over as some mentioned. Lifting the lid off slowly prevents air from blowing fine ground ☕ off the top edge of the removable lid. I spoon my grounds into the filter instead of trying to pour. Easier and prevents mess! Would buy again for sure! Update If you want grind a large amount I recommend putting a paper towel on the counter to keep grounds from the lid getting all over the counter. It makes it much easier to keep loose grounds from the lid from making a real mess!I’m a professional barista and this is the grinder I use everyday at home! It’s adjustable and holds enough for a good amount to grind. It’s easy to use and works great!!Very satisfied with this and great add to my morning routine. Two taps on the button and it grinds away at preset volume and fineness. Very convenient and easy to use. Much quieter and less "harsh" sounding than my older style grinder. Seeing other reviews, I've had no issues with spilled grounds - I tap the container on the counter a couple times before removing the lid. I also like the clean and functional Braun German design. Recommend.This is my third different burr coffee grinder of different manufacturers. I have now used it for a year. Flawless, excellent and uniform grind. Makes very fine espresso. Can grind up to 12 cups at a time, but the canister is large enough to hold twice that amount. Can be disassembled and cleaned easily. Ergonomics are perfect. For the price it is a relative bargain compared to other similar designs. Thus far this is my favorite grinder, and I do not expect to be looking for another for years. There has been no decrease in performance since I purchased it.Based on the prior customer reviews that I read about this mill, I would have passed on it but given my multi-year experience with the Braun KMM30 I decided to take a chance and ordered one. All I can say it that I'm glad I did. Have had none of the mess problems cited by other reviewers and find the mill is pretty quiet and quick at preparing my twice daily espresso grinds.
reviews_1s:
update 2:

The grinds hopper has a broken corner on it and I am unable to find replacement parts.

This grinder is a not able to do daily grinds and hold up. Poor quality PLASTIC...

The grinds reservoir also releases while in operation which wastes even more coffee.

I waste so much coffee with this imposter of a quality grinder. Every morning I end up with a mess of grounds all over my counter.

I'm going back to my little blade grinder, it has no bean hopper but it is efficient and does not waste my premium coffee beans.

Update:

I plan to replace this grinder as it wastes way too much coffee. I end up with grounds all over the place with this grinder. The ground coffee sticks to the grinds reservoir and it spills out when attempting to dump the grinds into my large french press. Then there's the pile of grinds that falls when you remove the reservoir. I thought this grinder would be convenient and easy when it's actually a high maintenance grinder with a lot of waste coffee grounds.
Very poor design.

It took a bit to get the settings dialed in for my french press. It seems to be calibrated to grind way more than the number of cups on the dial indicator.

The worst thing is the mess that this grinder makes. The ground coffee tray get static and the grinds stick to the sides making pouring the grounds a mess

The mess made when the grinds tray is removed is the biggest issue. There are always grinds that get into the try slot and they need top be cleaned out after each use.

I had one of these grinders before and the burr gears simply blew up one day.
I don't recall the messy aspect from my first unit.

This is NOT a grinder to put on your counter and simply use daily, it requires a lot of tending to clean and the wasted grounds add up.

I would make a firm pass on this grinder and get a better model.Play Video
Sorry Braun this thing sucks! Yes, I've read the other reviews. And most of the single star complaints are true. I had a different impression of Braun products, from their neat and compact shavers years ago, but this Braun is not that. So lets get with it. I make about 12-14 cups a coffee a day. That is for a family of three. We're heavy coffee drinkers. My method is Melita filter drip. I use med. roast beans, with a moderate oil base. I've been doing this for the last 25 years. My Capresso broke after years of dutiful service, that is when I decided to give this company a try. The price was not so much an incentive as I liked the look and feel of this appliance. My bad, I did not carefully read the reviews. Should have done that. That's the set up, now the product review. This product is priced less than a Capresso burr grinder, and there are clear differences. Yes, it is a burr grinder, but that's about where it stops. At the highest setting, my coffee grinds looks the same as my lowest setting - and that's a real problem when you doing a pour over, the filter constantly gets clogged because the grind is too fine. If I were making Expresso or Turkish Coffee like one of the 4 star reviewers is doing - well that's all good, but I am not. So, the range in the grind stinks. Next, and others have noted, get a sponge or a brush or some other micro particle pickup, cause you're going to need it, this grinder is messy, the electric static generated from the plastic requires a sweep and clean every time, picture included, this is every morning, another few minutes of labor. Also, the coffee stays clumpy stuck on the burrs spout, so a few loud taps on the side, and whole pounds with the grinder on the surface counter, and the appliance releases the rest of the staticky coffee. Nothing like waking up in the morning to struggle with a crappy form factor and thoughtless material design. Whoever approved this gadget is not a coffee maker, but an industrial designer not in touch with reality. Good for you Braun, you made one more disposable piece of trash for my local landfill. First and last product from you I purchase.I had a Braun burr grinder forever back in the 80's and 90's. (Yup, that old.) I was a fan of their excellent modern product design and it worked flawlessly. I bought this grinder with those warm and fuzzy memories. However, as other reviewers have said (why didn't I listen?) this machine is beyond a mess. We have grounds all across the counter and all over the sink EVERY time we use it. I can try to cover the opening with my hand and tap the grounds down, but they still explode all over when I remove the lid.

Beyond that, even though the machine is nice to look at, the hopper is too small. Bean refills are necessary every few days compared to the once every week to 10 days with the Capresso unit this replaced. And the grounds container is too big! The aforementioned Capresso unit's container was roughly the same size as a standard gold coffee filter, so the grounds could be dumped straight into the filter. This Braun machine's container is just too big, which exacerbates the exploding grounds problem. They just go everywhere, all over the top of the filter and then of course, down the outside of the filter and into the coffee pot. We are literally recycling this thing as we've struggled with it too long to return it. (We've been using it for 6 months.) I don't usually write reviews, but this product was so frustrating I felt the need to join the chorus to warn folks off of it!Far louder than the Bodum it replaced (better choice). Plastic container versus glass means that static builds during grinding and you'll have grinds all over the counter and your hands. 95% of the grinds go into the plastic container, with the rest ending up between the container and the housing. You'll constantly have to sweep the excess away before replacing the container, or it won't quite seal enough to trip the safety switch. It does periodically jam, and you'll have to give it a good jolt to let the beans flow again. The jolt usually results in tripping of the container-present safety switch resulting in a shut-down and a partially filled container with no way to "finish the aborted grind". With a scale of 1-10, 8 may be 25 seconds, so the numbers don't help you gauge "cups" or "seconds" if you're accustomed to one of those metrics to adjust strength. If they used a glass container, sealed it against the housing better, and used "seconds" as the indicator on the dial, it would be a much better product.I bought this machine a couple months ago. The first time I used it, I was bothered that some of the ground coffee stuck inside the receptacle container when I tried to pour it out. Static electricity.
I thought, 'No big deal -- it'll get better with use.' It has not gotten better with use.
One of the FAQ's on the company website suggests washing the container or the beans to help. Yes, that helped -- a little. Very little.
By now, I'm so displeased by the static cling of the coffee, I wish I had never bought the thing.
And the company even has an FAQ about the problem on their website -- so why haven't they fixed the problem? Maybe a different type of plastic? Aluminum? Grounding? I don't know, and I wish they would have solved the problem before selling the grinders.
I'm going to give this one to Goodwill and buy another brand.I received a USED grinder, i picked new option. Not only was it used the box came with coffee grounds covering everything. the manufacturer box that it came in looked liked it went through WW2. Not even that, it came in a huge box with no packing materials causing it to slide around making it worse. absolutely 0/10. absolutely insane for the priceThis is one annoying grinder. I have used it about 6 times since I bought it. After the first use, the grind changed to the finest possible and is no longer adjustable. Now I have to mix pre ground coffee to the stuff I put through this thing so that my coffee maker doesn't clog up and spill over. It is messy. Be prepared to clean a 3 foot perimeter around the grinding area. It is finicky and requires frequent unclogging of the spout where the coffee comes out. After I finish grinding my remaining beans, I am going to throw this in the trash and go back to buying pre ground coffee.If I could give this grinder a zero I would. It is the messiest grinder I have ever used and creates a strange static cling effect with the grounds and is not only impossible to clean but grounds get everywhere every time we use it. Due to the static effect, it also makes a mess of our coffee maker. I’m very surprised because Braun is an excellent company that makes quality products with good design but this is an utter failure. Already bought the OXO grinder as a replacement which is much neater and doesn’t have that static effect.I bought this grinder on 12/11/18. I submitted a good review prior to this because I was able to grind my beans to a size suitable for my K-cup brewer. It's 7/4/20: 2 weeks ago, the grind adjustment stopped working, and it only ground a size suitable for a french press. I cleaned it several times to no avail, and I removed and cleaned the burr as well. Nothing. A giant grind provides zero flavor in a K-cup brewer. So, this grinder went out with the garbage Wednesday-except for the plastic I could recycle. I have bought a new burr grinder from another company. I will not be reviewing it any time soon.I ignored the reviews that warned this item makes a dreadful mess of your countertop. What a fool I was. This Braun truly does fling coffee dust everywhere. As you remove the output chamber, you release coffee grinds. As you take the lid off the chamber, you release coffee grinds. There’s no comparison with my previous 15 year old Krupps. But now I don’t trust any electric burr grinder: I’ve bought a hand-crank which is very tidy indeed.
